seoTitle: Blackville vs Blair Athol — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest

ogTitle: Blackville vs Blair Athol: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Blackville and Blair Athol.
heading: Blackville vs Blair Athol — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Blackville, New South Wales and Blair Athol, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Blackville has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Blair Athol (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Blackville or Blair Athol safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Blackville has a flood risk score of 22/100, while Blair Athol scores 39/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Blackville scores 16/100 for bushfire risk. Blair Athol scores 51/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Blackville or Blair Athol?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Blackville has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.03% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
